claim: Semantic fallback insertion point is after matchTriggerPhrases results assembly at trigger-matcher.ts:855: call semanticTriggerFallback only when lexical results.length === 0 and SPECKIT_SEMANTIC_TRIGGERS is enabled.

No full re-index required: per-phrase memory_trigger_embeddings derived table reuses existing embedding-cache (content_hash, model_id, dimensions) keying; trigger phrases backfilled at save-time via embedding-pipeline.ts:143-169.

Semantic fallback algorithm: embed prompt once via generate_query_embedding, compute max cosine similarity per memory across its trigger phrase embeddings, filter at threshold 0.84 with 0.04 margin guard, cap at 3 results, return with importanceWeight: min(0.85, score) as cognitive activation guard.

Shadow mode logs semanticShadow telemetry field in TriggerMatchStats without including results in the returned list; promotion to union mode gated on shadow log analysis showing true-positive paraphrase recall with zero false-control-surface activations.

spec-folder-hierarchy.ts HierarchyTree is the direct PRAT analogue: buildHierarchyTree(db) provides ancestor/sibling structural scope expansion for trigger recall improvement without any embedding call; hierarchy-boost adds ancestor-folder cache entries at importanceWeight 0.6 after lexical match assembly.

Semantic fallback must be async and exposed via a new matchTriggerPhrasesHybrid entry point; existing synchronous matchTriggerPhrases and matchTriggerPhrasesWithStats remain unchanged to avoid breaking callers; latency budget at CONFIG.WARN_THRESHOLD_MS:30ms applies only to synchronous lexical path.
